description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant, cylindrical in shape with a domed top and prominent hexagonal operating nut at the very top. The hydrant features three hose connection outlets: two on opposite sides and one facing forward, each capped with a round metal cover. The body is segmented with raised bands and bolts, and the surface shows a matte, slightly rough finish typical of cast iron hydrants. The base is wider and flanged, designed for secure mounting to the ground, and the entire structure is painted in a uniform red color with some minor wear visible. No chains are attached to the caps, and there are no visible manufacturer or model markings on the exterior.
description: A red, cylindrical fire hydrant with a domed top, three capped hose outlets, and a matte, slightly rough finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main hose outlet and operating nut, with symmetrical side outlets visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one hose outlet and the cylindrical body, with the top nut and base flange visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the two side hose outlets and the smooth, unmarked back of the hydrant.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other hose outlet and the domed top, with the main outlet partially visible.
